title: noti configuration
description: Configuration schema for noti, a CLI tool that triggers notifications when a utility finishes.

$schema.description: URL of the JSON Schema for this configuration file.

defaults.description: Default notification services to enable when none are explicitly selected.

time.description: Show command execution time in the notification message.

icon.description: Path to notification icon image. On macOS, accepts PNG or JPEG. On Linux, accepts an image path or a freedesktop icon theme name. On Windows, accepts an .ico file path.

soundName.description: Banner success sound. See /System/Library/Sounds for available sounds.

soundNameFail.description: Banner failure sound. See /System/Library/Sounds for available sounds.

say.description: macOS say command speech notification settings.

voice.description: Name of voice used for speech notifications.

voiceName.description: Name of voice used for speech notifications.

voice.description: Name of voice used for speech notifications.

conversation.description: Keybase message destination. Can be either users (comma-separated) or team.

channel.description: Keybase team's chat channel to send to. Conversation must be a team. If empty, the team's default channel will be used (typically "general").

explodingLifetime.description: Keybase self-destructing message, after the specified time. Times are written like 30s (30 seconds), 15m (15 minutes), 24h (24 hours).

public.description: Enables broadcasting a message to everyone (when conversation is your username), or to teams (when conversation is your team name).

accessToken.description: Pushbullet access token. Log into your Pushbullet account and retrieve a token from the Account Settings page.

deviceIden.description: Pushbullet device iden of the target device, if sending to a single device.

apiToken.description: Pushover access token. Log into your Pushover account and create a token from the Create New Application/Plugin page.

userKey.description: Pushover message destination. Should be your User Key.

key.description: Pushsafer private or alias key. Log into your Pushsafer account and note your private or alias key.
Simplepush key. Install the Simplepush app and retrieve your key there.

token.description: Slack access token. Log into your Slack account and retrieve a token from the Slack Web API page.

channel.description: Slack message destination. Can be either a #channel or a @username.

token.description: Telegram access token. The token can be retrieved using the BotFather (https://telegram.me/botfather).

chatId.description: Telegram message destination. Can be either a chat or a channel.

topic.description: Telegram thread ID. If defined, the message will be posted in the thread; if omitted, the default thread will be used.

authToken.description: Twilio access token. Log into your Twilio account and copy the auth token from your project dashboard.

accountSid.description: Twilio account ID. Log into your Twilio account and copy the account SID from your project dashboard.

numberTo.description: Destination phone number for your SMS message. Format this number with a '+' and a country code, e.g., +16175551212.

numberFrom.description: The Twilio phone number, short code, or Messaging Service that sends this message. Must be a Twilio phone number that you own, formatted with a '+' and country code, e.g. +16175551212 (E.164 format).

template.description: Golang template used for producing the message text. It can include the title and message.

channelURL.description: Chanify sender webhook URL. Create one from the app.

sound.description: Turns on and off the sound (not in silent mode) when a notification is received.

priority.description: Priority number from 1 to 10 used by iOS to order and display relevant notifications.

interruptionLevel.description: How the notification appears (whether to show in DnD or not).

topic.description: Topic ID to send messages to.

token.description: Bearer access token for authentication on private topics. See https://docs.ntfy.sh/publish/#access-tokens.

key.description: Device key to send messages to.
Zulip bot API key. Create a bot in your Zulip organization and copy its API key.

to.description: Message destination. For "private": comma-separated list of recipient email addresses. For "stream": stream name.

username.description: Mattermost bot username displayed in the notification.

channel.description: Mattermost channel to post to (e.g., town-square). If empty, the webhook's default channel will be used.

incomingHookURI.description: Mattermost incoming webhook URL. Create one in your Mattermost instance under Integrations > Incoming Webhooks.

iconurl.description: URL of the icon to display for the bot. Overrides the webhook's default icon image.

type.description: Mattermost post type. Leave empty for standard messages. Can be used to set a custom post type (e.g., for plugin integrations).
